content:
# Numbers Chapter 3 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 148–150; Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 112–113 (LXX) | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/NUM_003_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_003_page_002.jpg`, `NUM_003_page_003.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/NUM_003_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_003_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

> **MANUSCRIPT OBSERVATION — THE LEVITES AND THE FIRSTBORN:** The Leningrad Codex continues on pages 148–150 with the genealogy and numbering of the sons of Levi, their assigned duties regarding the Tabernacle, and the substitution of the Levites for the firstborn of all Israel. This chapter stands apart from the census of chapters 1–2: the Levites are counted separately — from one month old and upward (מִבֶּן־חֹדֶשׁ, mi-ben-khodesh) rather than from twenty years — because they belong to YHWH in place of every firstborn. The Hebrew תּוֹלְדֹת (toledot, "generations") in v. 1 links this section to the priestly lineage. The Vaticanus LXX (pp. 112–113) follows the MT closely throughout, though it characteristically renders YHWH as κύριος and occasionally clarifies the Hebrew with small expansions. A significant textual crux arises at v. 39: the MT ketiv reads כָּל־פְּקוּדֵי הַלְוִיִּם... שְׁנַיִם וְעֶשְׂרִים אָלֶף (twenty-two thousand), yet the sum of the three clans (7,500 + 8,600 + 6,200) yields 22,300 — a discrepancy of three hundred that has occupied commentators since antiquity.

---

## The Generations of Aaron and Moses (vv. 1–4)

**1** And-these [ve-elleh] [are] the-generations of [toledot] Aaron [Aharon] and-Moses [u-Mosheh] in-the-day [be-yom] YHWH spoke [dibber] with [et] Moses [Mosheh] on-Mount [be-Har] Sinai [Sinai].

> **Key terms:** תּוֹלְדֹת (toledot) = "generations/begettings" — the same term used in Genesis to introduce genealogical sections. The heading says "Aaron and Moses" yet only Aaron's sons are listed; the rabbis note that whoever teaches Torah to another's children is as though he begat them — hence Moses is named. **LXX:** Αὗται αἱ γενέσεις Ααρων καὶ Μωυσῆ ("These are the generations of Aaron and Moses") — identical structure; κύριος for YHWH.

**2** And-these [ve-elleh] [are] the-names of [shemot] the-sons of [benei] Aaron [Aharon]: the-firstborn [ha-bekhor] Nadab [Nadav], and-Abihu [va-Avihu], Eleazar [El'azar], and-Ithamar [ve-Itamar].

> **LXX:** Ναδαβ ὁ πρωτότοκος καὶ Αβιουδ — Vaticanus has Αβιουδ for אֲבִיהוּא.

**3** These [elleh] [are] the-names of [shemot] the-sons of [benei] Aaron [Aharon], the-priests [ha-kohanim] the-anointed-ones [ha-meshukhim], whom [asher] he-filled [mille'] their-hand [et-yadam] to-serve-as-priest [le-khahen].

> **Key terms:** מִלֵּא אֶת־יָדָם (mille' et-yadam) = literally "he filled their hand" — the idiom for priestly ordination/consecration. The passive form "whose hand was filled" conveys investiture with priestly authority. **LXX:** οὓς ἐτελείωσαν τὰς χεῖρας αὐτῶν ("whose hands were consecrated") — τελειόω capturing the sense of מלא ידם.

**4** And-died [va-yamot] Nadab [Nadav] and-Abihu [va-Avihu] before [lifnei] YHWH in-their-bringing-near [be-haqrivam] fire [esh] strange [zarah] before [lifnei] YHWH in-the-wilderness of [be-midbar] Sinai [Sinai], and-sons [u-vanim] not [lo'] were [hayu] to-them [lahem]; and-served-as-priest [va-yekhahen] Eleazar [El'azar] and-Ithamar [ve-Itamar] upon [al] the-face of [penei] Aaron [Aharon] their-father [avihem].

> **Key terms:** אֵשׁ זָרָה (esh zarah) = "strange/unauthorized fire" — cf. Lev 10:1–2. עַל־פְּנֵי (al-penei) = "upon the face of" = "in the presence/lifetime of." **LXX:** πῦρ ἀλλότριον ("alien fire") for אש זרה; ἐναντίον Ααρων τοῦ πατρὸς αὐτῶν for על פני אהרן אביהם.

---

## The Levites Given to Aaron (vv. 5–10)

**5** And-spoke [va-yedabber] YHWH unto [el] Moses [Mosheh], saying [le'mor]:

**6** "Bring-near [haqrev] the-tribe of [et-matteh] Levi [Levi] and-stand [ve-ha'amadta] him [oto] before [lifnei] Aaron [Aharon] the-priest [ha-kohen], and-they-shall-serve [ve-sheretu] him [oto]."

> **Key terms:** הַקְרֵב (haqrev) = "bring near/present" — the same root (קרב) as sacrifice; the Levites are themselves a kind of offering. שֵׁרְתוּ (sheretu) = "they shall minister/serve" — the term for liturgical service. **LXX:** Λαβὲ τὴν φυλὴν Λευι... καὶ λειτουργήσουσιν αὐτῷ — λειτουργέω for שרת.

**7** "And-they-shall-keep [ve-shameru] his-charge [et-mishmarto] and-the-charge of [u-mishmeret] all [kol] the-congregation [ha-edah] before [lifnei] the-tent of [ohel] meeting [mo'ed], to-do [la'avod] the-service of [et-avodat] the-tabernacle [ha-mishkan]."

> **Key terms:** מִשְׁמֶרֶת (mishmeret) = "charge/guard-duty/obligation" — from שׁמר, "to guard." The Levites keep both Aaron's charge and the congregation's charge. **LXX:** φυλάξουσιν τὰς φυλακὰς αὐτοῦ — the doublet φυλάσσω/φυλακή mirrors שמר/משמרת.

**8** "And-they-shall-keep [ve-shameru] all [et-kol] the-vessels of [kelei] the-tent of [ohel] meeting [mo'ed] and-the-charge of [et-mishmeret] the-sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el], to-do [la'avod] the-service of [et-avodat] the-tabernacle [ha-mishkan]."

> **LXX:** καὶ φυλάξουσιν πάντα τὰ σκεύη τῆς σκηνῆς τοῦ μαρτυρίου — σκεύη for כלים; σκηνὴ τοῦ μαρτυρίου for אהל מועד.

**9** "And-you-shall-give [ve-natattah] the-Levites [et-ha-Levi'im] to-Aaron [le-Aharon] and-to-his-sons [u-le-vanav]; given [netunim] given [netunim] they [hemmah] to-him [lo] from-among [me'et] the-sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el]."

> **Key terms:** נְתוּנִם נְתוּנִם (netunim netunim) = "given, given" — the emphatic doubling stresses the absoluteness of their dedication. They are wholly given. **LXX:** δεδομένοι δεδομένοι — the Greek mirrors the Hebrew doubling exactly.

**10** "And-Aaron [ve-et-Aharon] and-his-sons [ve-et-banav] you-shall-appoint [tifqod], and-they-shall-keep [ve-shameru] their-priesthood [et-kehunnatam]; and-the-stranger [ve-ha-zar] who-comes-near [ha-qarev] shall-be-put-to-death [yumat]."

> **Key terms:** כְּהֻנָּה (kehunnah) = "priesthood" — the priestly office itself. זָר (zar) = "stranger/outsider" — here anyone not of Aaronic descent. **LXX:** ὁ ἀλλογενὴς ὁ προσπορευόμενος ἀποθανεῖται — ἀλλογενής ("foreign-born") for זר.

---

## The Levites as Substitute for the Firstborn (vv. 11–13)

**11** And-spoke [va-yedabber] YHWH unto [el] Moses [Mosheh], saying [le'mor]:

**12** "And-I [va-ani], behold [hinneh], I-have-taken [laqakhti] the-Levites [et-ha-Levi'im] from-among [mi-tokh] the-sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el] in-place-of [takhat] every [kol] firstborn [bekhor] — opener of [peter] womb [rekhem] — from-the-sons of [mi-benei] Israel [Yisra'el]; and-shall-be [ve-hayu] the-Levites [ha-Levi'im] to-me [li]."

> **Key terms:** פֶּטֶר רֶחֶם (peter rekhem) = "opener of womb" — the literal sense of "firstborn," the one who first opens the mother's womb. תַּחַת (takhat) = "in place of/instead of" — the substitutionary principle. **LXX:** ἀντὶ παντὸς πρωτοτόκου διανοίγοντος μήτραν — ἀντί for תחת; διανοίγω μήτραν for פטר רחם.

**13** "For [ki] to-me [li] [is] every [kol] firstborn [bekhor]; in-the-day [be-yom] I-struck [hakkoti] every [kol] firstborn [bekhor] in-the-land of [be-eretz] Egypt [Mitsrayim] I-consecrated [hiqdashti] to-me [li] every [kol] firstborn [bekhor] in-Israel [be-Yisra'el], from-man [me-adam] unto [ad] beast [behemah]; to-me [li] they-shall-be [yihyu]. I [am] YHWH."

> **Key term:** הִקְדַּשְׁתִּי (hiqdashti) = "I consecrated/set apart as holy" — the hiphil of קדשׁ. The firstborn became YHWH's possession at the Exodus. **LXX:** ἡγίασα ἐμοὶ πᾶν πρωτότοκον — ἁγιάζω for הקדיש.

---

## The Census of the Levites (vv. 14–20)

**14** And-spoke [va-yedabber] YHWH unto [el] Moses [Mosheh] in-the-wilderness of [be-midbar] Sinai [Sinai], saying [le'mor]:

**15** "Number [peqod] the-sons of [et-benei] Levi [Levi] by-the-house of [le-veit] their-fathers [avotam], by-their-clans [le-mishpekhotam]; every [kol] male [zakhar] from-a-son of [mi-ben] a-month [khodesh] and-upward [va-ma'lah] you-shall-number [tifqedem]."

> **Key terms:** מִבֶּן חֹדֶשׁ וָמָעְלָה (mi-ben khodesh va-ma'lah) = "from a son of a month and upward" — unlike the military census (twenty years), Levites are counted from one month old, underscoring their consecrated status from birth. **LXX:** ἀπὸ μηνιαίου καὶ ἐπάνω — "from a month old and above."

**16** And-numbered [va-yifqod] them [otam] Moses [Mosheh] upon [al] the-mouth of [pi] YHWH, as [ka'asher] he-was-commanded [tsuvvah].

> **Key terms:** עַל־פִּי יְהוָה (al-pi YHWH) = "upon the mouth of YHWH" = "at the command of YHWH." **LXX:** κατὰ τὸ ῥῆμα κυρίου — "according to the word of the Lord."

**17** And-were [va-yihyu] these [elleh] the-sons of [benei] Levi [Levi] by-their-names [bi-shemotam]: Gershon [Gershon] and-Kohath [u-Qehat] and-Merari [u-Merari].

> **LXX:** Γεδσων καὶ Κααθ καὶ Μεραρι — Vaticanus reads Γεδσων for גרשון (cf. Γηρσων in other MSS).

**18** And-these [ve-elleh] [are] the-names of [shemot] the-sons of [benei] Gershon [Gershon] by-their-clans [le-mishpekhotam]: Libni [Livni] and-Shimei [ve-Shim'i].

> **LXX:** Λοβενι καὶ Σεμεΐ.

**19** And-the-sons of [u-venei] Kohath [Qehat] by-their-clans [le-mishpekhotam]: Amram [Amram] and-Izhar [ve-Yitshar], Hebron [Khevron] and-Uzziel [ve-Uzzi'el].

> **LXX:** Αμβραμ καὶ Ισσααρ, Χεβρων καὶ Οζιηλ.

**20** And-the-sons of [u-venei] Merari [Merari] by-their-clans [le-mishpekhotam]: Mahli [Makhli] and-Mushi [u-Mushi]. These [elleh] they [hem] [are] the-clans of [mishpekhot] the-Levites [ha-Levi] by-the-house of [le-veit] their-fathers [avotam].

> **LXX:** Μοολι καὶ Μουσι.

---

## The Gershonites (vv. 21–26)

**21** To-Gershon [le-Gershon]: the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Libnites [ha-Livni] and-the-clan of [u-mishpakhat] the-Shimeites [ha-Shim'i]; these [elleh] they [hem] [are] the-clans of [mishpekhot] the-Gershonites [ha-Gershunni].

**22** Their-mustered-ones [pequdeihem] in-the-number of [be-mispar] every [kol] male [zakhar] from-a-son of [mi-ben] a-month [khodesh] and-upward [va-ma'lah] — their-mustered-ones [pequdeihem]: seven [shiv'at] thousands [alafim] and-five [va-khamesh] hundreds [me'ot].

> 7,500. **LXX:** ἑπτὰ χιλιάδες καὶ πεντακόσιοι — identical figure.

**23** The-clans of [mishpekhot] the-Gershonites [ha-Gershunni] — behind [akharei] the-tabernacle [ha-mishkan] they-shall-encamp [yakhanu] westward [yammah].

> **Key terms:** יָמָּה (yammah) = "seaward" = "westward" — in Hebrew, the Mediterranean Sea lies to the west. **LXX:** παρὰ τὴν σκηνὴν... πρὸς θάλασσαν — "beside the tabernacle... toward the sea."

**24** And-the-leader [u-nesi'] of-the-house-of [veit] the-father [av] of-the-Gershonites [la-Gershunni] — Eliasaph [Elyasaf] son of [ben] Lael [La'el].

> **LXX:** Ελισαφ υἱὸς Δαηλ — Vaticanus appears to read Δαηλ for לָאֵל.

**25** And-the-charge of [u-mishmeret] the-sons of [benei] Gershon [Gershon] in-the-tent of [be-ohel] meeting [mo'ed]: the-tabernacle [ha-mishkan] and-the-tent [ve-ha-ohel], its-covering [mikhsehu], and-the-screen [u-masakh] of-the-entrance of [petakh] the-tent of [ohel] meeting [mo'ed],

**26** and-the-hangings of [ve-qal'ei] the-court [he-khatser], and-the-screen of [u-masakh] the-entrance of [petakh] the-court [he-khatser] which [asher] [is] upon [al] the-tabernacle [ha-mishkan] and-upon [ve-al] the-altar [ha-mizbe'akh] around [saviv], and-its-cords [ve-et-meitarav] for-all [le-khol] its-service [avodato].

> **Key terms:** מִכְסֵהוּ (mikhsehu) = "its covering" — the outer covering of the tent. מָסָךְ (masakh) = "screen/curtain" — the entrance hangings. קַלְעֵי (qal'ei) = "hangings" of the court enclosure. מֵיתָרָיו (meitarav) = "its cords/ropes." **LXX:** τὰ ἱστία τῆς αὐλῆς for קלעי החצר; τοὺς κάλους αὐτῆς for מיתריו.

---

## The Kohathites (vv. 27–32)

**27** And-to-Kohath [u-li-Qehat]: the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Amramites [ha-Amrami] and-the-clan of [u-mishpakhat] the-Izharites [ha-Yitshari] and-the-clan of [u-mishpakhat] the-Hebronites [ha-Khevroni] and-the-clan of [u-mishpakhat] the-Uzzielites [ha-Uzzi'eli]; these [elleh] they [hem] [are] the-clans of [mishpekhot] the-Kohathites [ha-Qehati].

**28** In-the-number of [be-mispar] every [kol] male [zakhar] from-a-son of [mi-ben] a-month [khodesh] and-upward [va-ma'lah]: eight [shemonat] thousands [alafim] and-six [va-shesh] hundreds [me'ot] — keepers of [shomerei] the-charge of [mishmeret] the-holy-place [ha-qodesh].

> 8,600. **LXX:** ὀκτὼ χιλιάδες καὶ ἑξακόσιοι — identical figure. Note: MT reads שֵׁשׁ מֵאוֹת (six hundred). See textual note on the discrepancy at v. 39.

**29** The-clans of [mishpekhot] the-sons of [benei] Kohath [Qehat] shall-encamp [yakhanu] upon [al] the-side of [yerekh] the-tabernacle [ha-mishkan] southward [teimanah].

> **Key terms:** יֶרֶךְ (yerekh) = "thigh/flank/side" — the side or flank of the tabernacle. **LXX:** ἐκ πλαγίων τῆς σκηνῆς κατὰ λίβα — "on the sides of the tabernacle toward the south."

**30** And-the-leader [u-nesi'] of-the-house-of [veit] the-father [av] of-the-clans of [le-mishpekhot] the-Kohathites [ha-Qehati] — Elizaphan [Elitsafan] son of [ben] Uzziel [Uzzi'el].

> **LXX:** Ελισαφαν υἱὸς Οζιηλ.

**31** And-their-charge [u-mishmarttam]: the-ark [ha-aron] and-the-table [ve-ha-shulkhan] and-the-lampstand [ve-ha-menorah] and-the-altars [ve-ha-mizbekhot] and-the-vessels of [u-khlei] the-holy-place [ha-qodesh] which [asher] they-minister [yesharetu] with-them [ba-hem], and-the-screen [ve-ha-masakh] and-all [ve-khol] its-service [avodato].

> **Key terms:** The Kohathites are charged with the most sacred objects: the ark, the table [of showbread], the lampstand, the altars (plural — both the incense altar and the bronze altar), and the holy vessels. **LXX:** τὴν κιβωτόν... τὴν τράπεζαν... τὴν λυχνίαν... τὰ θυσιαστήρια.

**32** And-the-leader of [u-nesi'] the-leaders of [nesi'ei] the-Levites [ha-Levi] — Eleazar [El'azar] son of [ben] Aaron [Aharon] the-priest [ha-kohen] — [the] oversight of [pequddat] the-keepers of [shomerei] the-charge of [mishmeret] the-holy-place [ha-qodesh].

> **Key terms:** נְשִׂיא נְשִׂיאֵי הַלֵּוִי (nesi' nesi'ei ha-Levi) = "leader of the leaders of the Levites" — Eleazar holds the chief supervisory role over the Kohathites specifically and the Levites generally. פְּקֻדַּת (pequddat) = "oversight/appointment" — administrative charge. **LXX:** ὁ καθεσταμένος ἐπὶ τῶν φυλασσόντων τὰς φυλακὰς τῶν ἁγίων.

---

## The Merarites (vv. 33–37)

**33** To-Merari [li-Merari]: the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Mahlites [ha-Makhli] and-the-clan of [u-mishpakhat] the-Mushites [ha-Mushi]; these [elleh] they [hem] [are] the-clans of [mishpekhot] Merari [Merari].

**34** And-their-mustered-ones [u-fequdeihem] in-the-number of [be-mispar] every [kol] male [zakhar] from-a-son of [mi-ben] a-month [khodesh] and-upward [va-ma'lah]: six [sheshet] thousands [alafim] and-two-hundred [u-matayim].

> 6,200. **LXX:** ἓξ χιλιάδες καὶ διακόσιοι — Vaticanus reads ἓξ χιλιάδες καὶ πεντήκοντα (6,050) in some manuscripts, but the standard reading agrees with MT at 6,200.

**35** And-the-leader [u-nesi'] of-the-house-of [veit] the-father [av] of-the-clans of [le-mishpekhot] Merari [Merari] — Zuriel [Tsuri'el] son of [ben] Abihail [Avikhayil].

> **LXX:** Σουριηλ υἱὸς Αβιχαιλ.

**36** And-the-appointment of [u-fequddat] the-charge of [mishmeret] the-sons of [benei] Merari [Merari]: the-boards of [qarshe] the-tabernacle [ha-mishkan] and-its-bars [u-verikhav] and-its-pillars [ve-ammudav] and-its-sockets [va-adanav] and-all [ve-khol] its-vessels [kelav] and-all [ve-khol] its-service [avodato],

> **Key terms:** קְרָשִׁים (qerashim) = "boards/frames." בְּרִיחִים (berikhim) = "bars/crossbars." עַמּוּדִים (ammudim) = "pillars/posts." אֲדָנִים (adanim) = "sockets/bases." **LXX:** τὰς κεφαλίδας for the boards; τοὺς μοχλούς for the bars; τοὺς στύλους for the pillars; τὰς βάσεις for the sockets.

**37** and-the-pillars of [ve-ammudei] the-court [he-khatser] around [saviv] and-their-sockets [ve-adneihem] and-their-pegs [ve-yitdotam] and-their-cords [u-meitareihem].

> **Key terms:** יְתֵדֹת (yetedot) = "tent-pegs/stakes." **LXX:** τοὺς στύλους τῆς αὐλῆς κύκλῳ... καὶ τοὺς πασσάλους αὐτῆς καὶ τοὺς κάλους αὐτῆς.

---

## Those Encamping Before the Tabernacle (vv. 38–39)

**38** And-those-encamping [ve-ha-khonim] before [lifnei] the-tabernacle [ha-mishkan] eastward [qedmah], before [lifnei] the-tent of [ohel] meeting [mo'ed] toward-the-sunrise [mizrakhah] — Moses [Mosheh] and-Aaron [ve-Aharon] and-his-sons [u-vanav], keepers of [shomerei] the-charge of [mishmeret] the-sanctuary [ha-miqdash] for-the-charge of [le-mishmeret] the-sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el]; and-the-stranger [ve-ha-zar] who-comes-near [ha-qarev] shall-be-put-to-death [yumat].

> **Key terms:** The position of highest honor — eastward, before the entrance — belongs to Moses, Aaron, and Aaron's sons. The repeated warning: וְהַזָּר הַקָּרֵב יוּמָת (ve-ha-zar ha-qarev yumat) — "and the stranger who comes near shall be put to death." **LXX:** οἱ παρεμβάλλοντες κατὰ πρόσωπον τῆς σκηνῆς τοῦ μαρτυρίου ἀπ᾽ ἀνατολῆς.

**39** All [kol] the-mustered-ones of [pequdei] the-Levites [ha-Levi'im] whom [asher] numbered [paqad] Moses [Mosheh] and-Aaron [ve-Aharon] upon [al] the-mouth of [pi] YHWH by-their-clans [le-mishpekhotam], every [kol] male [zakhar] from-a-son of [mi-ben] a-month [khodesh] and-upward [va-ma'lah]: two [shenayim] and-twenty [ve-esrim] thousand [elef].

> 22,000. **CRITICAL TEXTUAL NOTE:** The MT reads שְׁנַיִם וְעֶשְׂרִים אֶלֶף (22,000), yet the three clan totals are: Gershon 7,500 (v. 22) + Kohath 8,600 (v. 28) + Merari 6,200 (v. 34) = 22,300. The discrepancy of 300 has been explained variously: (a) the 300 were themselves firstborn Levites who could not redeem other firstborn; (b) a scribal error in one of the subtotals (some suggest v. 28 originally read 8,300 — שְׁלֹשׁ מֵאוֹת for שֵׁשׁ מֵאוֹת); (c) the numbers serve a theological rather than arithmetic function. The LXX (Vaticanus) reads δύο καὶ εἴκοσι χιλιάδες (22,000), confirming the MT total. The Masorah notes "Aaron" here with a circule — the qere tradition may reflect awareness of the problem. **LXX also notes:** Vaticanus includes "and Aaron" (καὶ Ααρων) in this verse, aligning with the MT.

---

## The Census of the Firstborn (vv. 40–43)

**40** And-said [va-yomer] YHWH unto [el] Moses [Mosheh]: "Number [peqod] every [kol] firstborn [bekhor] male [zakhar] of-the-sons of [li-vnei] Israel [Yisra'el] from-a-son of [mi-ben] a-month [khodesh] and-upward [va-ma'lah], and-take [ve-sa'] the-number of [et mispar] their-names [shemotam]."

> **Key terms:** שָׂא אֶת מִסְפַּר שְׁמֹתָם (sa' et mispar shemotam) = "lift up the number of their names" — the idiom for a census count. **LXX:** λαβὲ τὸν ἀριθμὸν αὐτῶν κατ᾽ ὄνομα.

**41** "And-you-shall-take [ve-laqakhta] the-Levites [et-ha-Levi'im] for-me [li] — I [am] YHWH — in-place-of [takhat] every [kol] firstborn [bekhor] in-the-sons of [bi-vnei] Israel [Yisra'el], and-the-cattle of [et behemot] the-Levites [ha-Levi'im] in-place-of [takhat] every [kol] firstborn [bekhor] in-the-cattle of [bi-vhemat] the-sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el]."

> **Key terms:** The substitution extends even to livestock: Levitical cattle replace the firstborn cattle of all Israel. אֲנִי יְהוָה (ani YHWH) = "I [am] YHWH" — the divine self-identification formula, asserting authority. **LXX:** ἐγὼ κύριος for אני יהוה.

**42** And-numbered [va-yifqod] Moses [Mosheh], as-which [ka'asher] commanded [tsivvah] YHWH him [oto], every [et-kol] firstborn [bekhor] in-the-sons of [bi-vnei] Israel [Yisra'el].

**43** And-were [va-yihyu] all [kol] the-firstborn [bekhor] males [zakhar] in-the-number of [be-mispar] names [shemot] from-a-son of [mi-ben] a-month [khodesh] and-upward [va-ma'lah] of-their-mustered-ones [li-fequdeihem]: two [shenayim] and-twenty [ve-esrim] thousand [elef] three [sheloshet] and-seventy [ve-shiv'im] and-two-hundred [u-matayim].

> 22,273. **LXX:** δύο καὶ εἴκοσι χιλιάδες καὶ τρεῖς καὶ ἑβδομήκοντα καὶ διακόσιοι — identical figure (22,273). This means 273 firstborn exceed the number of Levites (22,273 − 22,000 = 273), requiring a monetary redemption for the surplus.

---

## The Exchange: Levites for Firstborn (vv. 44–48)

**44** And-spoke [va-yedabber] YHWH unto [el] Moses [Mosheh], saying [le'mor]:

**45** "Take [qakh] the-Levites [et-ha-Levi'im] in-place-of [takhat] every [kol] firstborn [bekhor] in-the-sons of [bi-vnei] Israel [Yisra'el], and-the-cattle of [ve-et-behemot] the-Levites [ha-Levi'im] in-place-of [takhat] their-cattle [behmtam]; and-shall-be [ve-hayu] the-Levites [ha-Levi'im] to-me [li]. I [am] YHWH."

> **LXX:** Λαβὲ τοὺς Λευίτας ἀντὶ πάντων τῶν πρωτοτόκων — ἀντί for תחת consistently.

**46** "And-[for] the-redeemed-ones [ve-et peduyei] of-the-three [ha-sheloshah] and-the-seventy [ve-ha-shiv'im] and-the-two-hundred [ve-ha-matayim] the-ones-exceeding [ha-odefim] over [al] the-Levites [ha-Levi'im] from-the-firstborn of [mi-bekhor] the-sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el] —"

> **Key terms:** פְּדוּיֵי (peduyei) = "the redeemed ones of" — from פדה, "to redeem/ransom." הָעֹדְפִים (ha-odefim) = "the ones exceeding/surplus" — the 273 firstborn for whom there is no Levite substitute. **LXX:** τὰ λύτρα τῶν... τῶν πλεοναζόντων — λύτρα ("ransom-price") for פדויי; πλεονάζω for עדף.

**47** "you-shall-take [ve-laqakhta] five [khameshet] five [khameshet] shekels [sheqalim] per-head [la-gulgoleth]; in-the-shekel of [be-sheqel] the-holy-place [ha-qodesh] you-shall-take [tiqqakh] — twenty [esrim] gerahs [gerah] the-shekel [ha-sheqel]."

> **Key terms:** חֲמֵשֶׁת חֲמֵשֶׁת שְׁקָלִים (khameshet khameshet sheqalim) = "five five shekels" — distributive: five shekels per person. לַגֻּלְגֹּלֶת (la-gulgoleth) = "per skull" = "per head/per capita." גֵּרָה (gerah) = a small weight, one-twentieth of a shekel. **LXX:** πέντε σίκλους κατὰ κεφαλήν... εἴκοσι ὀβολοὺς ὁ σίκλος — ὀβολός for גרה.

**48** "And-you-shall-give [ve-natattah] the-silver [ha-kesef] to-Aaron [le-Aharon] and-to-his-sons [u-le-vanav] — [the] redeemed-ones [peduyei] of-the-ones-exceeding [ha-odefim] among-them [ba-hem]."

> **LXX:** καὶ δώσεις τὸ ἀργύριον Ααρων καὶ τοῖς υἱοῖς αὐτοῦ, λύτρα τῶν πλεοναζόντων ἐν αὐτοῖς.

---

## The Redemption Money Collected (vv. 49–51)

**49** And-took [va-yiqqakh] Moses [Mosheh] the-silver of [et-kesef] the-redemption [ha-pidyom] from [me'et] the-ones-exceeding [ha-odefim] over [al] [the] redeemed-ones of [peduyei] the-Levites [ha-Levi'im].

> **Key terms:** פִּדְיוֹם (pidyom) = "redemption" — an alternate form of פדיון (pidyon). The money comes from those 273 firstborn who exceeded the Levite count and thus could not be redeemed by substitution. **LXX:** τὸ ἀργύριον τὰ λύτρα.

**50** From [me'et] the-firstborn of [bekhor] the-sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el] he-took [laqakh] the-silver [et-ha-kesef]: five [khamishah] and-sixty [ve-shishshim] and-three-hundred [u-shelosh me'ot] and-a-thousand [va-elef] in-the-shekel of [be-sheqel] the-holy-place [ha-qodesh].

> 1,365 [shekels]. Calculated: 273 persons x 5 shekels = 1,365. **LXX:** χίλιοι τριακόσιοι ἑξήκοντα πέντε σίκλοι — identical figure (1,365).

**51** And-gave [va-yitten] Moses [Mosheh] the-silver of [et-kesef] the-redeemed-ones [ha-peduyim] to-Aaron [le-Aharon] and-to-his-sons [u-le-vanav] upon [al] the-mouth of [pi] YHWH, as-which [ka'asher] commanded [tsivvah] YHWH Moses [et-Mosheh].

> **Key terms:** עַל־פִּי יְהוָה (al-pi YHWH) = "upon the mouth of YHWH" — the chapter ends as it proceeded: in obedience to the divine command. **LXX:** κατὰ τὸ ῥῆμα κυρίου — "according to the word of the Lord."

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

**1. The Toledot of Aaron and Moses (v. 1):** The heading reads "the generations of Aaron and Moses" yet lists only Aaron's sons. This is not a scribal error — the rabbinic tradition (b. Sanhedrin 19b) explains that Moses, as teacher, is credited with Aaron's progeny. The Vaticanus LXX preserves both names identically. The order "Aaron and Moses" (rather than the more common "Moses and Aaron") may reflect the priestly emphasis of the passage. — EMET

**2. The Death of Nadab and Abihu (v. 4):** The phrase אֵשׁ זָרָה (esh zarah, "strange fire") cross-references Leviticus 10:1–2. The Leningrad Codex is clear and unambiguous here. The note that "sons were not to them" (u-vanim lo' hayu lahem) explains why Eleazar and Ithamar alone continue the priestly line. The phrase עַל־פְּנֵי אֲבִיהֶם (al-penei avihem, "upon the face of their father") is deliberately ambiguous: it can mean "in the lifetime of" or "under the authority of" their father Aaron. — EMET

**3. The Emphatic Doubling netunim netunim (v. 9):** The Hebrew נְתוּנִם נְתוּנִם (netunim netunim) — "given, given" — is a rare emphatic construction stressing the completeness of the Levites' dedication. The LXX mirrors this with δεδομένοι δεδομένοι. This is not a scribal dittography; it is a deliberate rhetorical device. — EMET

**4. The 300 Discrepancy (v. 39):** The most significant textual problem in this chapter. The total of the three Levitical clans is: Gershon 7,500 (v. 22) + Kohath 8,600 (v. 28) + Merari 6,200 (v. 34) = 22,300. Yet v. 39 states the total as 22,000. The 300 discrepancy has been addressed as follows: (a) Rashi and the Talmud (b. Bekhorot 5a): the 300 surplus Levites were themselves firstborn, and a firstborn cannot redeem another firstborn — thus they are excluded from the substitution total; (b) some scholars posit a scribal corruption in v. 28, where שֵׁשׁ (six) may have replaced שְׁלֹשׁ (three), making the Kohathite total 8,300 and the grand total 22,000; (c) the Samaritan Pentateuch reads 22,000 for the total, agreeing with MT, though it too preserves the individual subtotals as they stand. The Vaticanus LXX reads 22,000 at v. 39, confirming the antiquity of this figure. EMET preserves the MT reading of 22,000 as received. — EMET

**5. Vaticanus Orthography:** The Vaticanus codex shows characteristic Greek forms: Γεδσων for Gershon (v. 17), Αβιουδ for Abihu (v. 2), Δαηλ for Lael (v. 24). These are transliteration variants, not substantive textual differences. — EMET

**6. The Shekel of the Holy Place (v. 47):** The phrase בְּשֶׁקֶל הַקֹּדֶשׁ (be-sheqel ha-qodesh) — "in the shekel of the holy place" — refers to the standard temple weight as opposed to the common commercial shekel. Its value is defined as twenty gerahs (עֶשְׂרִים גֵּרָה). The LXX renders gerah as ὀβολός (obol), adapting the measurement to a Hellenistic monetary context. — EMET

**7. The Redemption Calculation (v. 50):** The arithmetic is exact: 273 surplus firstborn x 5 shekels each = 1,365 shekels. This precision confirms the literary integrity of the passage: the surplus count (v. 46) and the shekel rate (v. 47) produce the exact figure stated in v. 50. — EMET

**8. The Form pidyom (v. 49):** The Leningrad Codex reads פִּדְיוֹם (pidyom), an alternate nominal form of the more common פִּדְיוֹן (pidyon, "redemption"). Both forms derive from the root פ-ד-ה. The final mem in pidyom may represent an archaic or dialectal variant. — EMET

---

*Translated from the Leningrad Codex (B19A) with reference to Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209). All 51 verses of Numbers Chapter 3 rendered word-for-word. — EMET*
